backlog.title: Provider lineup correction (remove EOL gemini; re-verify agy v1.1.0)
backlog.description: The built-in provider set reflects reality: gemini (Gemini CLI) is gone (superseded by agy on 2026-06-18); agy's manifest is corrected against its actual v1.1.0 flag surface (value-taking -p, no --approval-mode, --model replaces -m); every cross-reference (priority order, default-model table, package layout, assumptions, appendices, docs) agrees. The code-side work is already committed (2f77bd0, 010ecee, cdbccf5); this phase confirms the code matches the corrected spec and finishes the residual documentation drift.

milestones.title: Confirm gemini built-in removal from the built-in set and all references
milestones.description: The gemini (Gemini CLI) provider is no longer shipped. Confirm its removal from the provider registry, role defaults, TOML files, and all PRD cross-references. The word 'gemini' legitimately persists as model names (e.g. gemini-3.5-pro, Gemini 3.5 Flash) because agy runs the Gemini model family; those are correct and must NOT be touched.

tasks.title: Verify gemini is removed from the built-in provider set and all references
tasks.description: Confirm the code already matches the spec: exactly seven built-ins with no gemini entry, preferredBuiltins has no gemini, providers/gemini.toml does not exist, and the PRD §12.5 carries the REMOVED stub with clean cross-references.

subtasks.title: Verify code-side removal of the gemini built-in
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Architecture audit (plan/013_b8a415cc6e79/architecture/code_gemini_agy_audit.md) confirmed all four code checks PASS. This is a verification subtask; no code change is expected.
2. INPUT: The committed codebase at HEAD (cdbccf5).
3. LOGIC: Confirm four items:
   (a) `internal/provider/builtin.go` `BuiltinManifests()` returns exactly seven entries: pi, claude, agy, qwen-code, opencode, codex, cursor. No `builtinGemini` function exists. No `gemini` `Name:` entry.
   (b) `internal/provider/registry.go:15` `preferredBuiltins` is `["pi", "opencode", "cursor", "agy", "qwen-code", "codex", "claude"]` with no `gemini`.
   (c) `providers/gemini.toml` does NOT exist; `providers/` contains exactly 7 TOML files.
   (d) `internal/config/role_defaults.go` has no `gemini` key in the `roleDefaults` map.
   NOTE: Test fixtures in `internal/config/*_test.go` and `internal/cmd/models_test.go` that use "gemini" as arbitrary string data are NOT drift — they test config-resolution mechanics with opaque strings. Leave them alone.
4. OUTPUT: A one-line PASS/FAIL confirmation per item (a)–(d). If any item fails, restore the removed state per the spec (this would be unexpected — the audit confirmed all pass).
5. DOCS: none — pure code verification, no user-facing/config/API surface change.

subtasks.title: Confirm PRD §12.5 carries the REMOVED stub and cross-references are clean
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD.md was already updated in commit cdbccf5 ('Purge gemini-cli from PRD after built-in removal'). This subtask confirms that committed state.
2. INPUT: `PRD.md` at HEAD.
3. LOGIC: Confirm these PRD sections are consistent with gemini removal:
   (a) §12.5 (h3.57) reads '~~Built-in provider: Gemini CLI~~ — REMOVED (superseded by agy, §12.5.1)'.
   (b) §12 terminology list / fixed-backend examples: no `gemini`.
   (c) §6.1 G2 (h3.10): lists `agy` (not `gemini`) as one of the four shipped agents.
   (d) §9.16 FR-D1 (h3.32) cascading priority: `pi, opencode, cursor, agy, qwen-code, codex, claude` — no gemini.
   (e) §9.16 FR-D4 default-model table: no gemini row.
   (f) §14 package layout: no `providers/gemini.toml`.
   (g) Appendix D quick reference: no gemini row.
   (h) Appendix B.5 rescue example: uses agy, not gemini.
   NOTE: The word 'gemini' legitimately appears as a MODEL NAME (gemini-3.5-pro, Gemini 3.5 Flash) because agy runs the Gemini model family. These are correct.
4. OUTPUT: A one-line PASS/FAIL per item (a)–(h). This is already the committed state.
5. DOCS: The PRD edits themselves ARE this subtask's documentation surface (Mode A) — confirming the spec-of-record for the removal is the task.

milestones.title: Confirm the corrected agy manifest against agy v1.1.0 (2026-07-08)
milestones.description: agy diverged from the gemini-cli lineage in v1.1.0: -p became value-taking, --approval-mode was removed (replaced by --mode plan), and --model replaced -m. The corrected manifest is already committed (2f77bd0); confirm it matches the spec and the verification status is recorded.

tasks.title: Confirm the corrected agy manifest and verification status in code and PRD
tasks.description: Verify the agy manifest in builtin.go and providers/agy.toml matches §12.5.1 exactly, with the 2026-07-08 verification date recorded, and confirm PRD §12.5.1/§12.5.1.1/§22.1 reflect the re-verification.

subtasks.title: Verify the agy manifest fields in code match §12.5.1
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Architecture audit (code_gemini_agy_audit.md Check 1) confirmed all eight agy manifest fields MATCH at builtin.go:199-217. This is a verification subtask.
2. INPUT: `internal/provider/builtin.go` `builtinAgy()` (around line 199) and `providers/agy.toml`.
3. LOGIC: Confirm the manifest matches PRD §12.5.1 exactly:
   - `PrintFlag = strPtr("")` (non-nil empty) — agy v1.1.0's -p is value-taking; bare -p fails.
   - `ModelFlag = strPtr("--model")` — NOT `-m` (rejected in v1.1.0).
   - `PromptDelivery = "stdin"` — agy reads stdin when -p is absent.
   - `DefaultModel = "Gemini 3.5 Flash (Low)"` — agy models display label VERBATIM (reasoning suffix included).
   - `BareFlags = []string{"--mode", "plan"}`` — NOT `["--approval-mode", "default"]` (removed in v1.1.0).
   - `ListModelsCommand = []string{"agy", "models"}`.
   - `Experimental = true`.
   - `TooledFlags` nil (agy cannot serve as stager until §12.5.1.1 item 4 is resolved).
   Also confirm `internal/config/role_defaults.go` agy defaults use display labels: planner="Gemini 3.5 Flash (High)", message="Gemini 3.5 Flash (Low)", arbiter="Gemini 3.5 Flash (Medium)", stager="" (nil TooledFlags).
   The corrected render is: `agy --model "<label>" --mode plan < <sys+user payload via stdin>` (no -p).
4. OUTPUT: A one-line confirmation per field. All should PASS.
5. DOCS: The manifest comments in builtin.go/providers/agy.toml carry the 2026-07-08 verification record — confirm they are present and dated. This is the in-code documentation (Mode A).

subtasks.title: Confirm PRD §12.5.1/§12.5.1.1/§22.1 reflect the agy re-verification
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD.md was already updated in commit 2f77bd0 ('Re-verify and fix agy manifest against v1.1.0'). This confirms that committed state.
2. INPUT: `PRD.md` at HEAD.
3. LOGIC: Confirm:
   (a) §12.5.1 (h3.58) shows the corrected agy manifest (PrintFlag="", ModelFlag="--model", BareFlags=["--mode","plan"], etc.).
   (b) §12.5.1.1 (h4.0) marks items 1–3 as RESOLVED (2026-07-08, agy v1.1.0) and item 4 as OPEN (tooled/stager flags).
   (c) §22.1 risk table (h3.103) marks the agy non-TTY stdout drop (issue #76) as RESOLVED 2026-07-08.
   (d) §12.5.2 (qwen-code) notes that agy diverged from the gemini-cli lineage.
4. OUTPUT: A one-line PASS/FAIL per item (a)–(d). This is already the committed PRD state.
5. DOCS: The PRD sections themselves are this subtask's documentation surface (Mode A).

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B — residual drift fix)
milestones.description: The three implementation commits swept code, PRD, README.md, and partially docs/*.md, but did NOT fully reconcile docs/providers.md and docs/README.md with the corrected agy manifest and the removal of gemini. This milestone closes the documentation drift so the shipped docs are not stale. It is the ONLY milestone with actual file edits.

tasks.title: Fix residual docs/providers.md drift (agy table row, date, provider count)
tasks.description: Seven specific drift items in docs/providers.md: the agy quick-reference table row has two stale cells (print flag and tool-disable approach), the #76 verification date is stale, and the provider count '8' appears in four places where it should be '7'.

subtasks.title: Correct the agy quick-reference table row (print flag + tool-disable cells)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Architecture audit (docs_drift_audit.md items D1, D2) identified the exact drift. The agy row at docs/providers.md line 85 currently documents an invocation that NO LONGER WORKS on agy v1.1.0.
2. INPUT: `docs/providers.md` ~line 85 (the providers quick-reference table, agy row). Source of truth: `internal/provider/builtin.go:199-217` `builtinAgy()` and `providers/agy.toml`.
3. LOGIC: Edit the agy row in the quick-reference table. Two cells need correction:
   - **Print flag cell**: currently `` `-p` `` → change to ``(none)``. Rationale: agy v1.1.0's `-p`/`--print`/`--prompt` is value-taking (bare `-p` fails with 'flag needs an argument'); PrintFlag="" — agy reads stdin with no flag. Compare the opencode/codex rows which correctly say '(none)'.
   - **Tool-disable / bare essentials cell**: currently `` Read-only constraint (`--approval-mode default`) `` → change to `` Read-only constraint (`--mode plan`) ``. Rationale: `--approval-mode` was removed in agy v1.1.0; `BareFlags=["--mode","plan"]` is the read-only equivalent.
   Delivery (stdin), model flag (--model), default model (Gemini 3.5 Flash (Low)), sys-prompt (prepended), and stager (— no) cells are already correct — do NOT touch them.
4. OUTPUT: The agy row matches the corrected manifest exactly: `` | `agy` | stdin | (none) | `--model` | `Gemini 3.5 Flash (Low)` | (prepended) | Read-only constraint (`--mode plan`) | — no | ``
5. DOCS: This edit IS the documentation update (Mode A target file = docs/providers.md). The corrected table cell is the user-facing documentation of how agy is invoked.

subtasks.title: Fix verification date and provider count in docs/providers.md
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Architecture audit (docs_drift_audit.md items D3–D7) identified 5 drift items in docs/providers.md.
2. INPUT: `docs/providers.md` at the following lines. Source of truth: PRD §12.5.1 (verified 2026-07-08) and `builtin.go` (7 built-ins).
3. LOGIC: Five edits in `docs/providers.md`:
   (a) ~line 88 (note paragraph): change 'as of **2026-07-03**' → 'as of **2026-07-08**'. The manifest was re-verified on 2026-07-08 against agy v1.1.0. Optionally reframe that agy stays experimental only pending §12.5.1.1 item 4 (stager flags).
   (b) ~line 3: change 'the 8 built-in providers' → 'the 7 built-in providers'.
   (c) ~line 7: change 'Eight providers are compiled in as built-ins' → 'Seven providers are compiled in as built-ins'.
   (d) ~line 74: change heading '## The 8 built-in providers' → '## The 7 built-in providers'.
   (e) ~line 92: change 'The eight built-in providers achieve tool-safety' → 'The seven built-in providers achieve tool-safety'.
   The table at lines ~80-85 lists exactly 7 providers; these heading/prose '8's contradict the table itself.
4. OUTPUT: All '8'/'eight' provider-count references in docs/providers.md are '7'/'seven'; the verification date is 2026-07-08.
5. DOCS: These edits ARE the documentation updates (Mode A target file = docs/providers.md). Internal consistency between the table and its surrounding prose is the user-facing value.

tasks.title: Fix residual docs/README.md drift (provider count + field count)
tasks.description: The docs/README.md index entry for the providers page carries two stale values on the same line: provider count '8' (should be '7') and manifest schema field count '21' (should be '22').

subtasks.title: Correct provider count and field count in docs/README.md
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Architecture audit (docs_drift_audit.md items D8, D9) found this adjacent drift on the same line being edited. The field-count drift (21 → 22) is not strictly the 'provider lineup' correction but lives on the same line and is trivially fixable.
2. INPUT: `docs/README.md` ~line 35 (the 'Provider manifests' index row). Source of truth: `internal/provider/manifest.go` (22 toml tags) and `builtin.go` (7 built-ins).
3. LOGIC: Edit one line (~line 35) in `docs/README.md`:
   - Change 'the 8 built-in providers' → 'the 7 built-in providers'.
   - Change '21-field manifest schema' → '22-field manifest schema'. (manifest.go has 22 toml: tags; providers.md itself says '22-field schema'. docs/README.md is the lone outlier at 21.)
   Current line: `| [Provider manifests](providers.md) | 21-field manifest schema, command rendering, the 8 built-in providers (incl. agy and qwen-code), and adding a new agent. |`
   Corrected line: `| [Provider manifests](providers.md) | 22-field manifest schema, command rendering, the 7 built-in providers (incl. agy and qwen-code), and adding a new agent. |`
4. OUTPUT: The docs/README.md index row matches reality (7 providers, 22 fields).
5. DOCS: This edit IS the documentation update (Mode A target file = docs/README.md).

tasks.title: Final cross-reference grep + build/test smoke test (Mode B changeset-level verification)
tasks.description: The cross-cutting verification task: grep the full repo (excluding plan/) for stale gemini-as-built-in framing, confirm all docs/*.md and README.md enumerate seven built-ins, and run a smoke test (build + providers list/show agy) to confirm the shipped binary matches the corrected spec. This is the final gate that proves the whole delta is coherent.

subtasks.title: Comprehensive grep audit + build/test verification
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Architecture audit (docs_drift_audit.md §Summary) confirmed docs/cli.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/how-it-works.md, and README.md are CLEAN. This subtask verifies that remains true and that the P2.M3.T1/T2 edits introduced no regressions.
2. INPUT: The full repo (excluding `plan/` which contains immutable planning snapshots).
3. LOGIC: Three verification passes:
   (a) Grep: `git grep -in 'gemini' -- docs/ README.md providers/ internal/ pkg/` — every remaining hit must be either a MODEL NAME (e.g. gemini-3.5-pro, Gemini 3.5 Flash) or a LINEAGE/HISTORY comment (EOL, superseded, fork). Fix any that imply `gemini` is a shipped built-in provider.
   (b) Count check: confirm `docs/cli.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/how-it-works.md`, `docs/README.md`, `docs/providers.md`, and `README.md` do not list `gemini` as a built-in and do not say 'eight'/'8' providers.
   (c) Smoke test: `make build && ./bin/stagecoach providers list` — must list exactly seven built-ins with no `gemini`; `./bin/stagecoach providers show agy` — must print `print = ""` (or equivalent empty), `model_flag = "--model"`, and `--mode plan` in bare essentials. Then `go test ./internal/provider/... ./internal/config/... ./internal/cmd/...` must pass.
4. OUTPUT: Grep is clean of stale gemini-as-built-in framing; providers list/show agy match the spec; all targeted test suites pass.
5. DOCS: Mode B final verification — confirms the whole delta is coherent across PRD, code, manifests, and docs. This is the catch-all that prevents a coherent delta from shipping with stale references.
